mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a trailing three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration, triggering equity risk-off and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate trigger condition is satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate and CPI inflation captures the ex-ante real policy stance; when the funds rate remains low relative to inflation, negative real rates stimulate borrowing and equity revaluation, generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no incremental explanatory power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 20-day returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals reviving consumer demand and revenue visibility for cyclical companies, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank Information Coefficient above 0.04 during the 2010 through 2013 period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) anticipate construction sector expansion and downstream employment multiplier effects, which increase aggregate income expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST do not align with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor income and household spending power, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound eliminates money-market yields and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional historical average when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, measured by a t-test with p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales captures a demand-supply virtuous cycle where rising output meets strengthening consumption, amplifying corporate profit expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of INDPRO and RSAFS does not predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
